result:
Boids simulation created and verified.

Files:
- index.html (links style.css in <head>, includes <script src="boids.js"></script> as the last element in <body>)
- style.css (full-viewport dark background, no scrollbars)
- boids.js (plain JS, no modules/imports; sizes canvas, spawns 350 boids, implements separation/alignment/cohesion, colorful triangle rendering, resize handling, and requestAnimationFrame loop)

Wiring and static checks passed:
- All files exist.
- <canvas id="boids"> present.
- <link rel="stylesheet" href="style.css"> present.
- <script src="boids.js"></script> present and last in body; no type="module".
- boids.js references the canvas, gets 2D context, handles resize, initializes ~350 boids, and animates/draws each frame.
- No ES module syntax detected.

How to run:
- Open index.html in a modern browser. You should see hundreds of fast, colorful boids flocking on the full-window canvas.
